author: Professor Johnson
text: How is everyone finding the difficulty level of this course so far?

author: Alice
text: The material is challenging but manageable if I stay on top of it.

author: Bob
text: I agree. I think the pace is okay, but the assignments take longer than expected.

author: Carol
text: I disagree a bit. I actually wish some topics went deeper instead of moving on so quickly.

author: Dan
text: Maybe a better compromise is this: The readings help, but I still feel behind by the end of each week.

author: Jack
text: That suggestion would solve the issue for a lot of students.

author: Eve
text: From my experience, maybe optional advanced practice would help students who want more rigor.

author: Frank
text: The material is challenging but manageable if I stay on top of it.

author: Grace
text: I agree. I think the pace is okay, but the assignments take longer than expected.

author: Henry
text: I disagree a bit. I actually wish some topics went deeper instead of moving on so quickly.

author: Noah
text: I think that concern matters more than people realize.

author: Alice
text: Can we talk about the grading for Homework 2? The rubric felt unclear.

author: Dan
text: I lost points for formatting even though my reasoning was correct.

author: Eve
text: I agree. The rubric felt too vague for something worth that many points.

author: Frank
text: I disagree a bit. Fast feedback would matter more to me than tiny rubric details.

author: Grace
text: Maybe a better compromise is this: A checklist at the top of the assignment would make grading feel more fair.

author: Olivia
text: This is why I support changing the policy.

author: Henry
text: From my experience, i think the grader focused too much on presentation instead of understanding.

author: Isabella
text: I lost points for formatting even though my reasoning was correct.

author: Jack
text: I agree. The rubric felt too vague for something worth that many points.

author: Karen
text: I disagree a bit. Fast feedback would matter more to me than tiny rubric details.

author: Sam
text: That suggestion would solve the issue for a lot of students.

author: Leo
text: Maybe a better compromise is this: A checklist at the top of the assignment would make grading feel more fair.

author: Bob
text: Should participation in discussion count more toward the final grade?

author: Grace
text: I think participation should include online responses, not just speaking in class.

author: Henry
text: I agree. Increasing participation weight could disadvantage quieter students.

author: Isabella
text: I disagree a bit. Forum posts would be a better way to measure engagement fairly.

author: Jack
text: Maybe a better compromise is this: A broader definition of participation would make this feel more inclusive.

author: Tina
text: That is exactly why clearer expectations would help.

author: Karen
text: From my experience, i like participation points when they reward preparation rather than volume.

author: Leo
text: I think participation should include online responses, not just speaking in class.

author: Maya
text: I agree. Increasing participation weight could disadvantage quieter students.

author: Noah
text: I disagree a bit. Forum posts would be a better way to measure engagement fairly.

author: Xavier
text: This is why I support changing the policy.

author: Olivia
text: Maybe a better compromise is this: A broader definition of participation would make this feel more inclusive.

author: Paul
text: From my experience, i like participation points when they reward preparation rather than volume.

author: Carol
text: Can we move the project checkpoint deadline by two days?

author: Jack
text: A short extension would really help teams still debugging deployment.

author: Karen
text: I agree. I would rather reduce the scope than move the deadline again.

author: Leo
text: I disagree a bit. Many groups planned around the original schedule, so changing it now could be messy.

author: Maya
text: Maybe a better compromise is this: The checkpoint would feel more reasonable with narrower requirements.

author: Yara
text: This is basically the same problem I ran into last week.

author: Noah
text: From my experience, a two-day shift could make a big difference for teams balancing other exams.

author: Olivia
text: A short extension would really help teams still debugging deployment.

author: Paul
text: I agree. I would rather reduce the scope than move the deadline again.

author: Quinn
text: I disagree a bit. Many groups planned around the original schedule, so changing it now could be messy.

author: Clara
text: That is exactly why clearer expectations would help.

author: Rita
text: Maybe a better compromise is this: The checkpoint would feel more reasonable with narrower requirements.

author: Sam
text: From my experience, a two-day shift could make a big difference for teams balancing other exams.

author: Tina
text: A short extension would really help teams still debugging deployment.

author: Dan
text: Would it be possible to record review sessions for students who cannot attend live?

author: Maya
text: Office hours fill up too quickly before deadlines.

author: Noah
text: I agree. An evening slot would help students who have labs during the day.

author: Olivia
text: I disagree a bit. More office hours before exams would lower stress a lot.

author: Paul
text: Maybe a better compromise is this: I have found office hours useful, but the current schedule is too limited.

author: Derek
text: I think that concern matters more than people realize.

author: Quinn
text: From my experience, could we add one virtual session each week for commuting students?

text: Office hours fill up too quickly before deadlines.

author: Xavier
text: I agree. An evening slot would help students who have labs during the day.

author: Eve
text: What do people think about adding more office hours before the midterm?

author: Paul
text: Recorded review sessions would help students with conflicts.

author: Quinn
text: I agree. Worked examples are more useful than just posting slides afterward.

author: Rita
text: I disagree a bit. Accessibility alone feels like a strong reason to record them.

author: Sam
text: Maybe a better compromise is this: Recordings would help people revisit confusing parts before exams.

author: Ian
text: That suggestion would solve the issue for a lot of students.

author: Tina
text: From my experience, i would especially value recordings if they included problem walkthroughs.

author: Frank
text: How useful have the discussion boards been for understanding the material?

author: Sam
text: The discussion board is helpful when people post concrete examples.

author: Tina
text: I agree. It becomes much less useful when questions go unanswered for days.

author: Uma
text: I disagree a bit. Pinned summary answers would make the board easier to navigate.

author: Victor
text: Maybe a better compromise is this: I learn a lot from seeing how other students phrase questions.

author: Nina
text: This is why I support changing the policy.

text: From my experience, searchability matters because similar questions keep coming up.

author: Henry
text: We mostly struggle with integration, not with individual tasks.

author: Isabella
text: Our biggest blocker is that frontend and backend assumptions keep drifting apart.

author: Jack
text: A short check-in on API expectations would probably save us hours.

author: Karen
text: I think the checkpoint should focus on end-to-end functionality, even if styling is incomplete.

author: Leo
text: We also lost time because the API quota blocked repeated testing.

author: Maya
text: A written sample dataset with expected outputs would make debugging easier.

author: Noah
text: The thread map works, but we still need better evidence that it helps more than normal chat.

author: Olivia
text: A larger dataset would make the usefulness of the graph much clearer in the demo.

author: Paul
text: We should show one messy thread in chat and then switch to graph to make the contrast obvious.

author: Quinn
text: Topic filtering is probably the next most practical improvement after timeline filtering.

author: Rita
text: Clickable insight cards would make the interface feel smarter without overbuilding it.

author: Sam
text: A most controversial thread card would give the graders something instantly understandable.

author: Tina
text: I also think summary panels should point users toward what to inspect next.

author: Uma
text: The live demo needs a clearer story from overview to evidence.
